rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BOURGES VS. DANY SCHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Plaintiff'S Vacate Judgement Defendant'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgement On Sanctions Based On Ccp 473.B And Cr 3.110(F) Declaration Of Additional Evidence Fifth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES Motion For Plaintiff's Vacate Judgement Defendant's 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgement On Sanctions Based On CCP 473.B And Cr 3.110(F) Declaration Of Additional Evidence Fifth Amended Complaint. Motions to Vacate are DENIED. The motions are untimely and in any event do not show mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. Defendant's request for sanctions regarding these motions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LORI RYAN VS. MAX KNIGHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave For Neurosurgical Examination Of Plaintiff Lori Ryan
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday April 10, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MAX KNIGHT, RICHARD KNIGHT, BEVERLY KNIGHT'S Motion For Leave For Neurosurgical Examination Of Plaintiff Lori Ryan. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A NEUROSURGICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE FOR A SECOND EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED IN HER DEPOSITION, WHICH WAS IN ADVANCE OF THE FIRST EXAMINATION, THAT SHE WAS GOING TO HAVE THE RHIZOTOMY PROCEDURE TO RELIEVE THE PAIN DUE TO HER ALLEGED INJURIES AND THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT SHE IS NOW CLAIMING DAMAGES FOR A SEPARATE CONDITION. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 10, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI VS. EKO VENTURE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Motion To Compel Psychiatric Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT EKO VENTURE, INC. Defendants Motion To Compel Psychiatric Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff. Motion is GRANTED. Doyle v. Superior Court (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 476 holds that a plaintiff's mental condition is not "in controversy" within the meaning of CCP §2032.020 if she is not claiming "ongoing" emotional distress. However, the Court has been provided no executed stipulation, other agreement, or amendment to the complaint showing that the plaintiff here is no longer claiming ongoing emotional distress. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JIN YAN ZHU ET AL VS. JESSICA H LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE Motion For Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed. The motions to compel arbitration and stay proceedings are both DENIED. The "arbitration agreement" (actually a sentence in a document called "Corporate Bond") purports to require arbitration by the Chinese government. The "agreement" is unconscionable and unenforceable for several reasons: (1) it is take-it-or-leave-it boilerplate; (2) the title "Corporate Bond" is misleading; (3) the arbitration rules were not attached or otherwise provided; indeed, defendant's papers give no indication that even her counsel knows what the rules are. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHANNON DEATON ET AL VS. MARK CHANEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 10, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT MARK CHANEY, CHANEY CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled. General demurrer will be sustained under the doctrine of Res Judicata where the facts alleged in the complaint or matters judicially noticed show plaintiff to be seeking relief from the same defendant on the same cause of action as in a prior action. [Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 C4th 788, 792, 108 CR3d 806, 808.] The issues and parties in the case are bar are not the same as the previous case. The complaint must be "liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." [CCP § 452; see Stevens v. Sup.Ct. (API Auto Ins. Services) (1999) 75 CA4th 594, 601, 89 CR2d 370, 374]. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
